rulings.Case Title: CONNIE CREWS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 06, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA WELDING SUPPLY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFFS' TESTIMONY DESCRIBES PRODUCTS PURCHASED FROM DEFENDANT AND USED BY DECEDENT INCLUDING A "LINCOLN" WELDING ROD. DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF WILL BE UNABLE TO PROVE THOSE PRODUCTS LACKED ASBESTOS AS PLAINTIFFS HAVE ASSERTED IN RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW FERNANDEZ VS. PAULO MAFOA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 06, 2008 LINE 6. PLAINTIFF ANDREW FERNANDEZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED EXCEPT AS TO DEFENSES ONE AND THIRTEEN. PLAINTIFF SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE DOES NOT RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BECAUSE COMMISSIONER CHAN'S ORDER OF DECEMBER 11, 2007 BARS DEFENDANT FROM OFFERING EVIDENCE UNTIL THERE HAS BEEN COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ONE AND TWO ARE SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. DANIEL Y WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Martin Eng'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Modify Order Granting Motion For Summary Judgment And For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 06, 2008 LINE 16. PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S AMENDED MOTION TO MODIFY ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED ON THE COMPLAINT IN NOVEMBER 2007 AND MUST BE VACATED BEFORE COURT COULD EVEN CONSIDER THIS MOTION. M.B. V CITY OF SAN DIEGO (1991) 233 CAL APP 3D 699, 707. =(302/PJM/LB)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK AZAD VS. BETH WILLIAMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 06, 2008 LINE 9. PLAINTIFF FRANK AZAD'S Motion To File Amended ComplainT IS DENIED AS TO NGUYEN. PLAINTIFF, FIRST, MUST SET ASIDE THE ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT COUPLED WITH THE REQUEST TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. ANY SUCH MOTION SHOULD BE HEARD BY JUDGE KAHN. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE OTHER DEFENDANTS, NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BURTON VS. FREDERICK P. FURTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Dfs' Anti-Slapp Special Mtn To Strike Pl'S 1st Cause Of Action For Libel, Request For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 06, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANTS FURTH FIRM, L.L.P., CHALK HILL ESTATE VINEYARDS, L.L.C., CHALK HILL ESTATE WINERY, L.L.C. AND FREDERICK FURTH'S Anti-Slapp Special MOTION To Strike PLAINTIFF'S FIRST Cause Of Action For Libel (Request For Judicial NOTICE): THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE LIBEL CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 425.16. THE E-MAIL IS A WRITTEN STATEMENT ISSUED IN CONNECTION WITH AN ISSUE UNDER JUDICIAL REVIEW. CONTEMPORARY SERVICE CORP. V STAFFPRO INC. (2007) 152 CAL APP 4TH 1043. HAVING SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF, THE COURT REVIEWS PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT DO SO BECAUSE BOTH THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE AND THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE BAR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. CIVIL CODE SECTION 47; HEALY V TUSCAN HILLS LANDSCAPE & RECREATION CORP. (2006) 137 CAL APP 4TH 1, 5-6; DEAILE V GENERAL TELEGRAPH CO. (1974) 40 CAL APP 3D 841, 847. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE MALICE SO AS TO NEGATE THE PRIVILEGE. REGARDING DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS, THE COURT NEED NOT RULE ON EACH ONE GIVEN THE COURT'S RULING ON THE PRIVILEGES. ALTHOUGH THERE IS SOME HEARSAY IN MS. BURTON'S STATEMENT, THE VAST MAJORITY OF THE OBJECTIONS ARE WITHOUT MERIT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARK WILLIAMS VS. VIA LEE LOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Be Declared Prevailing Party And For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 06, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANTS PAUL WARTELLE, FRANCIS PINNOCK AND WARTELLE, WEAVER & SCHREIBER'S MOTION To Be Declared Prevailing Party And For An Award Of Attorney'S Fees IS GRANTED. THE FEES SOUGHT, $14,000 AND THE COSTS, $1,045, ARE REASONABLE AND THE FEES ARE BASED ON MARKET RATES. WITTE V KAUFMAN (2006) 141 CAL. APP 4TH 1201 IS NOT CONTROLLING. BECAUSE THE FIRM OF WARTELLE, ET AL. IS A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATATION, IT MAY RECOVER FOR THE WORK OF ONE OF ITS EMPLOYEES. P.L.C.M. GROUP, INC. V DREXLER (2000) 22 CAL 4TH 1084. THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS HAD AN ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WITH THE LAW CORPORATION AND ATTORNEYS WEAVER AND MCDONALD. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RESTAURANTS INTERNATIONAL, INC. A CORPORATION et al VS. NANCY MAR et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 06, 2008 LINE 15. DEFENDANTS NANCY MAR AND 1660 SACRAMENTO STREET ASSOCIATION, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS ONLY AGAINST MAR AND MAR'S REQUEST TO FILE A DEMURRER IS DENIED. OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. ALTHOUGH THE CLAIM AGAINST MAY BE BARRED, IT DOES NOT MEAN THE DEBT NO LONGER EXISTS OR THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT SEEK TO RECOVER FROM OTHERS. THE ISSUES RELATED TO THE BANKRUPTCY FILING RAISE QUESTIONS OF FACT BEYOND THE SCOPE OF THE COMPLAINT. TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. =(302/PJM/PB)
